trajectory_summary: The call was truncated by an operational blocker — outdated satellite imagery — that the rep treated as a hard stop rather than a single data gap to work around. Don moved from distracted to genuinely engaged (volunteering garage details, confirming tree removal) before the rep exited. The proximate cause of truncation was the rep's decision to defer everything to a callback rather than continuing qualification on the information already available.
outcome_dispute: v1 outcome tag is unknown. Based on the transcript, the call ended with a vague callback promise ('I can give you a callback once I have more updated imagery') — no locked time, no appointment, no qualification completed beyond address and a thin interest answer. This is a soft-callback outcome, not a booked appointment. No-show risk on the callback is high given the absence of a locked time and the rep's failure to complete any verify items beyond address.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Imagery gap as call-stopper vs. qualification continues
primary_coaching_focus.quote: And then I can give you a callback once I have more updated imagery. Does that work for you?
primary_coaching_focus.why: Tyson treated an operational data gap (outdated satellite imagery) as a reason to end the call, leaving five verify items uncollected (bill, utility, co-owners, credit, roof age) and zero button_up elements executed. The coaching question is whether Tyson understands that imagery is a design-team input, not a qualification prerequisite. Walk through the verify section and ask him to identify what he could have collected after the imagery gap surfaced — then walk through all six button_up elements and what a locked callback looks like vs. 'does that work for you?'

key_moments.quote: Hey, Don. This is Tyson Ion. I received a request to look at solar for the home.
key_moments.stewart_read: The rep named Ion and referenced the solar request, which is a partial legitimacy anchor. However, the schema intro template calls for Tesla/state/certified-installer framing to answer 'is this a scam?' immediately. That framing was absent. The gap is the specific legitimacy scaffold, not a total absence of company context — Ion was named, a design-framing followed at 00:19. Strategic-vs-reactive: no evidence this was a deliberate choice to skip the Tesla frame; the reactive read holds — the rep defaulted to a lighter intro without the strongest available legitimacy signal.

key_moments.quote: We've thought about it before. We just when this came up saying that they need needed people to test them. Maybe.
key_moments.stewart_read: Don's answer to 'what got you interested in solar?' is thin and hedged — 'maybe' is the closing word. The schema flags this as the emotional anchor of the call and calls for 60+ seconds of depth-probing. The rep's follow-up ('is this kind of your first time fully getting a design') is a binary clarifier, not a depth probe. The rep checked the box and moved on. This is not a knowledge_gap (the rep lacking an answer) — it is a depth-probe miss on a thin customer answer that needed expansion.

key_moments.quote: There's nothing in this is there's a new house in the house. And it would be going on the garage, not my house.
key_moments.stewart_read: Don volunteers two high-value signals in one breath: (1) it's a new construction, meaning the roof is new and solar-ready, and (2) the install target is the garage, not the main house — a detail that changes the design scope entirely. The rep acknowledged the new-home detail and asked a clarifying question, which is adequate. What the rep did NOT do is anchor on the new-roof signal as a positive qualifier or use the garage detail to deepen curiosity ('a garage install is actually a great setup — let me make sure we get the right design for that'). The enthusiasm signal was received but not amplified.

key_moments.quote: Yeah. The trees are not even there.
key_moments.stewart_read: Don confirms the trees are gone — the shading concern the rep raised is eliminated. This is a second enthusiasm signal: improved sun exposure on a new-construction garage roof. The rep's response was to exit the call on a callback promise rather than treating this as a green light to continue qualification. The imagery gap is real, but it does not block collection of bill, utility, co-owners, credit, or roof age — all of which were still available.
